annotations.label: Kalman filtering

anchor.snippet: Kalman filtering (also known as linear quadratic estimation ) is an algorithm that uses a series of measurements observed over time

annotations.note: No Kalman filter formalization exists in Mathlib4 (grep for `Kalman` returned no results).

annotations.label: Optimality under Gaussian noise

anchor.snippet: Optimality of Kalman filtering assumes that errors have a normal (Gaussian) distribution

annotations.note: No Kalman filter or its optimality theorem appears in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Best linear estimator (MMSE)

anchor.snippet: if the process and measurement covariances are known, then the Kalman filter is the best possible linear estimator in the minimum mean-square-error sense

annotations.note: MMSE optimality of Kalman filter is not formalized in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Truck position with GPS and dead reckoning

anchor.snippet: As an example application, consider the problem of determining the precise location of a truck

annotations.note: This applied example has no Mathlib4 formalization.

annotations.label: Kalman filter as recursive estimator

anchor.snippet: The Kalman filter is an efficient recursive filter estimating the internal state of a linear dynamic system from a series of noisy measurements

annotations.note: No Kalman filter definition in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Underlying linear dynamic system model

anchor.snippet: Kalman filtering is based on linear dynamic systems discretized in the time domain

annotations.note: Discrete-time linear stochastic state-space models are not formalized in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: State transition equation

anchor.snippet: The Kalman filter model assumes the true state at time

annotations.note: No state-transition equation of a Kalman model in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Observation equation

anchor.snippet: an observation (or measurement)

annotations.note: No Kalman observation model in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Mutual independence of initial state and noise

anchor.snippet: The initial state, and the noise vectors at each step

annotations.note: General mutual independence of random variables exists, but the specific Kalman noise-independence assumption is not formalized.

annotations.label: Recursive estimator (filter state notation)

anchor.snippet: The Kalman filter is a recursive estimator

annotations.note: Not present in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Predict and update phases

anchor.snippet: it is most often conceptualized as two distinct phases

annotations.note: Kalman predict/update phases are not formalized in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Joseph form of covariance update

anchor.snippet: The second formula for the updated ( a posteriori ) estimate covariance above is known as the "Joseph form"

annotations.note: Joseph form covariance update is not in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Invariants of the Kalman filter estimates

anchor.snippet: If the model is accurate, and the values for

annotations.note: Kalman invariants (unbiasedness, etc.) are not formalized.

annotations.label: Autocovariance least-squares (ALS) estimation

anchor.section: Estimation of the noise covariances Q k and R k
anchor.snippet: One practical method of doing this is the autocovariance least-squares (ALS) technique

annotations.note: ALS noise covariance estimation is not in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Optimal state estimation conditions

anchor.snippet: The Kalman filter provides an optimal state estimation in cases where

annotations.note: Optimality conditions for the Kalman filter are not formalized.

annotations.label: Whiteness of innovation sequence

anchor.snippet: If the Kalman filter works optimally, the innovation sequence (the output prediction error) is a white noise

annotations.note: Innovation whiteness theorem is not in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Truck on frictionless rails

anchor.snippet: Consider a truck on frictionless, straight rails

annotations.note: Applied example not present in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Convergence of gain matrices to asymptotic value

anchor.snippet: Convergence of the gain matrices

annotations.note: Asymptotic Kalman gain convergence is not formalized.

annotations.label: Asymptotic Kalman filter as LTI filter

anchor.snippet: the Kalman filter becomes a linear time-invariant filter

annotations.note: Steady-state LTI Kalman filter is not in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Discrete Riccati equation for asymptotic covariance

anchor.snippet: can be computed by first solving the following discrete Riccati equation for the asymptotic state covariance

annotations.note: Grep for `Riccati` returned no matches in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Kalman filter as generalized least squares

anchor.snippet: The Kalman filter can be derived as a generalized least squares method operating on previous data

annotations.note: GLS derivation of Kalman filter is not in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Derivation of posteriori covariance (Joseph form)

anchor.section: Deriving the posteriori estimate covariance matrix
anchor.snippet: This formula (sometimes known as the Joseph form of the covariance update equation) is valid for any value

annotations.note: Joseph form derivation is not formalized.

annotations.label: Optimal Kalman gain (MMSE)

anchor.snippet: This gain, which is known as the optimal Kalman gain , is the one that yields MMSE estimates when used

annotations.note: Optimal Kalman gain theorem is not in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Simplified posteriori covariance with optimal gain

anchor.section: Simplification of the posteriori error covariance formula
anchor.snippet: The formula used to calculate the a posteriori error covariance can be simplified when the Kalman gain equals the optimal value

annotations.note: Simplified covariance update is not formalized.

annotations.label: Sensitivity recursion under misspecified statistics

anchor.snippet: This sensitivity analysis describes the behavior of the estimation error covariance when the noise covariances

annotations.note: Kalman sensitivity analysis is not in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Cholesky (square-root) factorization of covariance

anchor.snippet: Positive definite matrices have the property that they have a factorization into the product of a non-singular , lower-triangular matrix

annotations.note: Grep for `Cholesky` returned no results in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: U-D decomposition form

anchor.snippet: An equivalent form, which avoids many of the square root operations

annotations.note: U-D decomposition is not in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: L·D·L^T square-root filter

anchor.snippet: decomposition of the innovation covariance matrix

annotations.note: LDL^T square-root Kalman variant is not in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Parallel (prefix-sum) Kalman filter complexity reduction

anchor.snippet: it is however possible to express the filter-update routine in terms of an associative operator

annotations.note: Parallel-scan Kalman filter not in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Markov conditional independence of true state

anchor.snippet: Because of the Markov assumption, the true state is conditionally independent of all earlier states given the immediately previous state

annotations.note: Generic conditional independence exists, but the specific Markov-chain conditional independence is not formalized.

annotations.label: Conditional independence of measurements

anchor.snippet: the measurement at the

annotations.note: General conditional independence machinery exists; the specific Kalman measurement independence is not formalized.

annotations.label: Predict and update as probability distributions

anchor.snippet: This results in the predict and update phases of the Kalman filter written probabilistically

annotations.note: Bayesian Kalman predict/update probabilistic form is not formalized.

annotations.label: Kalman filter as generative model

anchor.snippet: the Kalman filter can be viewed as a generative model

annotations.note: Generative-model view of Kalman is not in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Marginal likelihood

anchor.snippet: This probability is known as the marginal likelihood

annotations.note: Grep for marginal likelihood returned no matches in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Recursive log marginal likelihood update

anchor.snippet: to avoid numeric underflow , in a practical implementation it is usually desirable to compute the log marginal likelihood

annotations.note: Recursive log-likelihood update is not formalized.

annotations.label: Information filter (inverse covariance form)

anchor.snippet: In the information filter, or inverse covariance filter, the estimated covariance and estimated state are replaced by the information matrix and information vector

annotations.note: Information filter is not in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Information filter summation update for N measurements

anchor.snippet: The main advantage of the information filter is that N measurements can be filtered at each time step simply by summing their information matrices and vectors

annotations.note: Information filter summation property is not formalized.

annotations.label: Optimal fixed-lag smoother

anchor.snippet: The optimal fixed-lag smoother provides the optimal estimate of

annotations.note: Fixed-lag smoother is not in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Optimal fixed-interval smoother (Kalman smoothing)

anchor.snippet: The optimal fixed-interval smoother provides the optimal estimate of

annotations.note: Fixed-interval Kalman smoothing is not in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Rauch–Tung–Striebel smoother

anchor.snippet: The Rauch–Tung–Striebel (RTS) smoother is an efficient two-pass algorithm for fixed interval smoothing

annotations.note: RTS smoother is not in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Modified Bryson–Frazier smoother

anchor.snippet: the modified Bryson–Frazier (MBF) fixed interval smoother developed by Bierman

annotations.note: Grep for `Bryson|Frazier` returned no matches.

annotations.label: Minimum-variance smoother

anchor.snippet: The minimum-variance smoother can attain the best-possible error performance

annotations.note: Minimum-variance smoother is not formalized.

annotations.label: Causal part equals minimum-variance Kalman filter

anchor.snippet: Taking the causal part of this minimum-variance smoother yields

annotations.note: Causal-part equivalence to Kalman filter is not formalized.

annotations.label: Frequency-weighted Kalman filter

anchor.snippet: a frequency shaping function is used to weight the average power of the error spectral density in a specified frequency band

annotations.note: Frequency-weighted Kalman filter is not in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Extended Kalman filter

anchor.snippet: In the extended Kalman filter (EKF), the state transition and observation models need not be linear functions of the state but may instead be nonlinear functions

annotations.note: EKF is not in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Unscented Kalman filter

anchor.snippet: The unscented Kalman filter (UKF)

annotations.note: UKF is not in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Sigma points and weights

anchor.snippet: sigma points are any set of vectors

annotations.note: Sigma points (unscented transform) are not in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: UKF predict step

anchor.snippet: The sigma points are propagated through the transition function

annotations.note: UKF predict step not formalized.

annotations.label: UKF update step

anchor.snippet: These sigma points are transformed through the measurement function

annotations.note: UKF update step not formalized.

annotations.label: Discriminative Kalman filter

anchor.snippet: When the observation model

annotations.note: Discriminative Kalman filter is not in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Adaptive Kalman filter

anchor.snippet: Adaptive Kalman filters allow to adapt for process dynamics which are not modeled in the process model

annotations.note: Adaptive Kalman filter is not in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Kalman–Bucy filter

anchor.snippet: Kalman–Bucy filtering (named for Richard Snowden Bucy) is a continuous time version of Kalman filtering

annotations.note: Continuous-time Kalman–Bucy filter is not in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Covariance differential equation is Riccati

anchor.snippet: The second differential equation, for the covariance, is an example of a Riccati equation

annotations.note: Matrix Riccati ODE is not in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Hybrid Kalman filter model

anchor.snippet: Most physical systems are represented as continuous-time models while discrete-time measurements are made frequently for state estimation

annotations.note: Hybrid continuous/discrete Kalman filter is not formalized.

annotations.label: Hybrid filter predict step

anchor.snippet: The prediction equations are derived from those of continuous-time Kalman filter without update from measurements

annotations.note: Hybrid filter predict step is not formalized.

annotations.label: Hybrid filter update step

anchor.snippet: The update equations are identical to those of the discrete-time Kalman filter

annotations.note: Hybrid filter update step is not formalized.

annotations.label: Sparse-signal recovery Kalman variant

anchor.section: Variants for the recovery of sparse signals
anchor.snippet: The traditional Kalman filter has also been employed for the recovery of sparse , possibly dynamic, signals from noisy observations

annotations.note: Sparse-signal Kalman variant is not in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Kalman filter as Gaussian process regression solver

anchor.snippet: Since linear Gaussian state-space models lead to Gaussian processes, Kalman filters can be viewed as sequential solvers for Gaussian process regression

annotations.note: Gaussian processes are defined in Mathlib, but the Kalman-as-GP-regression viewpoint is not formalized.
